class.name: FmodAudioEffect
class.brief_description: Abstract base class for audio effects in the FMOD audio system.
class.description: FmodAudioEffect is the abstract base class for all audio effects in the FMOD integration. It provides a common interface for applying effects to audio buses (channel groups). Subclasses implement specific effect types such as Amplify, Filter, EQ, Reverb, Delay, etc. The effect can be applied to a [FmodChannelGroup] using [method apply_to] and removed using [method remove_from_bus].

method.name: apply_to
method.description: Applies this effect to the specified audio bus. Subclasses should override this method to add their DSP units to the bus.

method.name: create_custom_dsp
method.description: Creates a custom DSP unit for this effect. Subclasses can override this to provide custom DSP behavior.

method.name: get_bus
method.description: Gets the audio bus (channel group) that this effect is currently applied to.

method.name: get_dsp_chain
method.description: Gets the chain of DSP units created by this effect.

method.name: get_dsp_description
method.description: Gets the DSP description structure for creating custom DSPs. Subclasses can override this to customize DSP behavior. Returns a pointer to FMOD_DSP_DESCRIPTION.

method.name: remove_from_bus
method.description: Removes this effect from the specified audio bus, detaching all DSP units.

members.member: The audio bus (channel group) that this effect is applied to.
The array of DSP units created by this effect.

signal.name: changed
signal.description: Emitted when the effect's parameters have changed and need to be reapplied.
